DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID NEMECEK'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES BY SUGARMADE, INC. AND JIMMY CHAN TO FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, FOR FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION TO BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN FULL. NO OPPOSITION FILED. OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER, DEFENDANTS TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE. REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE (REQUEST NOS. 1-13) ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $70.00, TO BE PAID WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM NOAH, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance By Sugarmade, Inc. And Jimmy Chan With Court Order, For Evidentiary, Issue And Monetary Sanctions And For Order Striking Defendants Answer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAVID NEMECEK's Motion To Compel Compliance By Sugarmade, Inc. And Jimmy Chan With Court Order, For Evidentiary, Issue And Monetary Sanctions And For Order Striking Defendants Answer To Complaint

Motion DENIED. Because the verification attached to Defendants' papers predated the Order of Feb. 10, 2020, it cannot have verified the amended responses. Therefore, the present motion is timely. CCP 2030.300(c); 2031.310(c); 2033.290(c); Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 635-36. However, because neither Plaintiff's declaration nor the Reply indicate any effort to meet and confer after receipt of the amended responses, I conclude that terminating or issue sanctions under CCP 128(a)(4) would be excessive. This is because meet and confer efforts may have remedied the problems identified in the motion. See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771 (discussing factors to consider in exercise of discretion, including whether additional time could remedy problems). Regarding the motion to strike/for issue sanctions under the CCP 2023.010, 2023.030, again, I find that meet and confer efforts may have remedied any problems. Regarding Civil Code 177.5, that section requires a finding that the party potentially subject to sanctions acted without good cause or substantial justification, and without a record on meeting and conferring, I cannot determine that Defendants acted without substantial justification. This order does not resolve whether the amended responses are compliant with either the Civil Discovery Act or the Court's prior order. The parties should meet and confer about any deficiencies in the amended responses and file appropriate motions if those discussions are not fruitful.

order signed.
Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

And Jimmy Chan To First Set Of Requests For Production, First Set Of Form Interrogatories And First Set Of Special Interrogatories, For First Set Of Requests For Admission To Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. Within 10 days of notice of entry of order, Defendants to serve verified responses to Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, and Special Interrogatories, Set One. Requests for Admission, Set One (Request Nos. 1-13) are deemed admitted. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $70.00, to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order.
